primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer, en comptes consolidés, quelles familles de relations de couverture IFRS peuvent théoriquement accueillir la composante de risque de change liée à un dividende intragroupe lorsqu’une créance correspondante a été comptabilisée.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge accounting eligibility, hedged item, and hedge model questions; therefore IFRS 9 governs and IAS 39 is discarded.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the question is about hedge accounting eligibility/models and IFRS 9 expressly governs those matters for current IFRS reporting.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements and identify the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain when intragroup monetary items generate foreign exchange effects in consolidated financial statements and how net investment related exchange differences are presented and recycled.
This Interpretation clarifies the hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge model within a group context.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk
Hedge accounting models for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk
Hedge accounting models for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability or a component thereof exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk associated with a recognised item or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 21 treat the hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate named hedge accounting model, further clarified by IFRIC 16 for group situations.
